Carnations have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia, due to their sedative and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of carnations include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with carnations include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate digestive discomfort.
